title: 1. Three Principles of Cosmetic Acupuncture
text:
Cosmetic acupuncture treatment consists of three principles.
①Systematic effect with qi modulation (qi and Meridian)
②Local effect with needle stimulation (Skin tissue)
③Balancing with muscle release (Muscle/fascia)
The ﬁrst one is based on traditional Chinese medicinal theories. You are controlling the
face by controlling the internal organs and meridian. Second, many treatment modalities use
the stimulation of the dermis itself. Roller needles, needling on the dermis at the wrinkle
location,  and  transplant  needle  methods  fall  into  this  category.  The  third  principle  is
balancing face symmetry by adjusting muscle tensegrity. In the actual clinical ﬁeld, all of
these methods are used without much distinction.
When you put the needles on ST-4, your purpose can be regulating the Stomach Meridian
(the 1st principle), and you may have wanted to make an intentional small scar right under
the nasolabial fold (the 2nd principle). Or your intention was releasing the orbicularis oris or
the zygomaticus major (the 3rd principle). The same point can be used for all three reasons.
What is really important is the way you are putting needles in that certain spot. If your
intention is muscle releasing, the needle should be inserted deep enough to target the
muscle. If your intention is skin stimulation, inserting the needle at the dermis layer with
horizontal needling would be the best approach. The needling method, size of the needle,
and manipulation can be different from what you intended, and a proper method can
increase the effectiveness of your treatment.

Example : Three ways of insertion at ST-7
①Systematic effect with qi modulation (qi and Meridian)
If you insert the needle in ST-7 for the regular channel stimulation, perpendicular
angle with 10-15mm insertion depth with ﬁne needle is recommended.
②Local effect with needle stimulation (Skin tissue)
If your purpose is increasing the circulation of the dermis for treating scar of acne,
transverse angle will be optimal, or you can use the roller needles to stimulate the
dermis.
③Balancing with muscle release (Muscle/ fascia)
If your purpose is releasing the lateral pterygoid muscle, perpendicular-oblique
angle (about 70 degree toward the nose tip) with 30-40mm insertion depth is
recommended.
* These three ways of insertion are used at the same point, ST-7, but the angle of
insertion, the insertion depth, and the size of needle can differ based on the
purposes.
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
